LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT, BUT ON ITS OWN MOTION, ADVANCED PLAINTIFF'S NOTICED MOTION TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT FROM JANUARY 13, 2009 TO JANUARY 8, 2009. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-26-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Lipton of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4218) for defendant and moving party
Oliver McFarline Phillips (no phone number given) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence and plaintiff failed to present evidence that creates a triable issue of fact. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. The Court denied plaintiff's oral request for leave to file a supplemental complaint, but on its own motion, advanced plaintiff's noticed motion to file a supplemental complaint from January 13, 2009 to January 8, 2009. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
